did_this_paper_use_ai_to_obtain_results_.details: The paper developed a hybrid deep neural network (HDNN) system for early skin disease detection and classification. It utilized EfficientNetB7 for classification of five skin conditions (Melanoma, Basal Cell Carcinoma, Melanocytic Nevi, Benign Keratosis-like Lesions, and Seborrheic Keratoses) and YOLOv8 for detection. The study achieved an accuracy of 94% with EfficientNetB7 and a mean average precision (mAP) of 0.812 with YOLOv8.

is_this_paper_a_review_paper_or_a_resear.details: The paper presents original experimental work developing a hybrid deep neural network (HDNN) system integrating EfficientNetB7 and YOLOv8 for early skin disease detection. It reports specific performance metrics (94% accuracy, mAP 0.812), describes the methodology including dataset sources (Kaggle and Primary Data), and implements a web-based system, distinguishing it from a review or survey.

title: Developing an early detection model for skin diseases using a hybrid deep neural network to enhance health independence in coastal communities
brief_summary: This study developed a hybrid deep neural network (HDNN) system integrating EfficientNetB7 for classification and YOLOv8 for detection to enable early skin disease diagnosis in resource-limited coastal communities. The model was trained on datasets accessed via Kaggle and primary data, covering five specific skin conditions: Melanoma, Basal Cell Carcinoma, Melanocytic Nevi, Benign Keratosis-like Lesions, and Seborrheic Keratoses. The classification task utilized a balanced dataset of 5,000 images, while the detection task employed 3,000 images categorized by malignancy. Evaluation results demonstrated that EfficientNetB7 achieved an accuracy of 94% on test data, and YOLOv8 attained a mean average precision (mAP) of 0.812 for detection. The researchers implemented the system as a web-based platform to provide an accessible, user-friendly tool for healthcare providers and the public. The findings suggest that this hybrid approach effectively enhances diagnostic efficiency and accessibility for underserved populations.

lesion_types_studied.details: One of the five skin conditions classified by the EfficientNetB7 model. Included in the balanced dataset of 5,000 images with 1,000 images per class. Also categorized under 'Cancer' for the YOLOv8 detection task.

One of the five skin conditions classified by the EfficientNetB7 model. Included in the balanced dataset of 5,000 images with 1,000 images per class. Described as a common type of skin cancer often caused by excessive sun exposure. Categorized under 'Cancer' for the YOLOv8 detection task.

One of the five skin conditions classified by the EfficientNetB7 model. Included in the balanced dataset of 5,000 images with 1,000 images per class. Described as small spots or generally benign moles. Categorized under 'Non-cancer' for the YOLOv8 detection task. The paper notes difficulty in distinguishing melanoma and black nevus due to similar lesion size and color.

One of the five skin conditions classified by the EfficientNetB7 model. Included in the balanced dataset of 5,000 images with 1,000 images per class. Described as a group of skin changes that resemble keratosis but are non-cancerous. Categorized under 'Non-cancer' for the YOLOv8 detection task.

paper_terms: Seborrheic Keratoses and other benign tumors
lesion_types_studied.details: One of the five skin conditions classified by the EfficientNetB7 model. Included in the balanced dataset of 5,000 images with 1,000 images per class. Described as benign skin tumors commonly found in the elderly. Categorized under 'Non-cancer' for the YOLOv8 detection task.

all_task_types.target: Five specific skin disease categories: Melanoma, Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC), Melanocytic Nevi (NV), Benign Keratosis-like Lesions (BKL), and Seborrheic Keratoses and other benign tumors.
all_task_types.details: The EfficientNetB7 model performs multiclass classification of five distinct skin conditions using a balanced dataset of 5,000 images (1,000 per class). The primary metric reported is accuracy (94%). Additionally, the system assesses malignancy risk (cancer vs. non-cancer) as part of the classification/detection workflow.

all_task_types.target: Localization of skin lesions categorized into two groups: Cancer and Non-cancer.
all_task_types.details: The YOLOv8 model performs object detection to locate lesions and categorize them as malignant or benign. It was trained on a dataset of 3,000 images (1,500 cancer, 1,500 non-cancer). The primary metric reported is mean average precision (mAP) of 0.812.

datasets_used.name: Skin Diseases Image Dataset (Kaggle)
datasets_used.description: A public dataset accessed via the Kaggle platform, containing images of five specific skin disease classes used for the classification task.

use.training: Used for training the EfficientNetB7 classification model. The paper reports using data sharing scenarios of (8:1:1), (7:1.5:1.5), and (5:2.5:2.5) for train, test, and validation splits.
use.validation: Used for validation of the EfficientNetB7 classification model based on the reported splits.
use.testing: Used for testing the EfficientNetB7 classification model based on the reported splits.

datasets_used.name: Primary Data (Coastal Area Collection)
datasets_used.description: Private dataset collected directly from research locations in coastal areas through direct photography of individuals, used for the object detection task.

use.training: Used for training the YOLOv8 detection model.
use.validation: Used for validation of the YOLOv8 detection model.
use.testing: Used for testing the YOLOv8 detection model.

statistical_methods_used.purpose: To categorize lesion sizes (small, medium, large) based on the first (Q1) and third (Q3) quartiles of the area distribution derived from bounding box dimensions.
To assess the overlap between predicted bounding boxes and ground truth boxes, serving as a metric for detection accuracy and a threshold for determining correct predictions (e.g., IoU ≥ 0.5).
To filter overlapping bounding boxes by retaining the one with the highest confidence score and removing others if their IoU exceeds a certain threshold.

statistical_methods_used.method: Confusion Matrix Analysis (implied by Accuracy, Precision, Recall, F1-score)
statistical_methods_used.purpose: To evaluate classification performance metrics (accuracy, precision, recall, F1-score) for the EfficientNetB7 model and detection performance (mAP) for the YOLOv8 model.

hypothesis_testing.interpretation: The paper did not report any formal hypothesis testing, p-values, or significance tests. Performance was evaluated using descriptive metrics such as accuracy (94%), mean average precision (mAP), precision, recall, and F1-score, along with functional black-box testing.

Skin Diseases Image Dataset (Kaggle).description: Balanced dataset of 5,000 images (1,000 per class) for classification of Melanoma, BCC, NV, BKL, and Seborrheic Keratoses.

experiments.name: Scenario 1 (8:1:1 split)
experiments.notes: Model 1 achieved the most balanced and stable performance with no significant overfitting.

experiments.name: Scenario 2 (7:1.5:1.5 split)
experiments.notes: Model 2 showed slightly higher training accuracy but experienced a drop in validation performance, indicating potential overfitting.

experiments.name: Scenario 3 (5:2.5:2.5 split)
experiments.notes: Model 3 showed slightly higher training accuracy but experienced a drop in validation performance, indicating potential overfitting.

Primary Data (Coastal Area Collection).description: Dataset of 3,000 images (1,500 Cancer, 1,500 Non-cancer) for object detection.

experiments.notes: Used as a baseline comparison for the larger dataset performance.

experiments.split_details: Not explicitly split in results table, but derived from full primary data
experiments.notes: The detection model benefited from the larger dataset, showing increased mAP compared to the 1,500-image subset. The overall reported mAP for the system is 0.812.

architecture_used.main_model: Hybrid Deep Neural Network (HDNN) integrating EfficientNetB7 for classification and YOLOv8 (specifically YOLOv8m medium) for detection.
architecture_used.pretraining: EfficientNetB7 was pre-trained on the ImageNet dataset. YOLOv8m was initialized as a pre-trained model.
architecture_used.input_size: Not explicitly reported (EfficientNetB7 input layer adjusted to specific resolution; YOLOv8 input size not specified).

classification_model.name: EfficientNetB7
classification_model.added_layers: Dense layers with ReLU activation function for non-linearity; Softmax layer for output probability distribution.

detection_model.name: YOLOv8
detection_model.backbone: Convolutional Neural Network (CNN) for feature extraction.
detection_model.head: Bounding box prediction using anchor boxes of various sizes; classification head for object class.

training_details.loss_function: Categorical crossentropy (for classification); Loss function for detection not explicitly named but implied by YOLOv8 standard.
training_details.data_augmentation: Rotation Range, Width Shift Range, Height Shift Range, Shear Range, Zoom Range, Horizontal Flip, Fill Mode.

classification_or_output_head.classification: Softmax layer generating class probability distributions for 5 classes.
classification_or_output_head.detection: Bounding box coordinates (x, y, w, h) and confidence scores.

preprocessing_techniques: Data annotation using Roboflow platform (adding bounding boxes and labels)
Image resizing to consistent dimensions (224×224 pixels for EfficientNetB7; 640×640 pixels for YOLOv8)
Normalization of pixel values to the range [0, 1]
Data augmentation techniques: Rotation Range, Width Shift Range, Height Shift Range, Shear Range, Zoom Range, Horizontal Flip, and Fill Mode
Dataset splitting into Training, Validation, and Testing sets

conclusions: The developed hybrid deep neural network (HDNN) system, integrating EfficientNetB7 for classification and YOLOv8 for detection, effectively addresses the need for early skin disease diagnosis in resource-limited and underserved coastal communities.

The EfficientNetB7 model achieved a classification accuracy of 94% on test data for five specific skin conditions: Melanoma, Basal Cell Carcinoma (BCC), Melanocytic Nevi (NV), Benign Keratosis-like Lesions (BKL), and Seborrheic Keratoses.
The YOLOv8 model achieved a mean average precision (mAP) of 0.812 for detecting skin lesions and assessing malignancy risk (cancer vs. non-cancer).
The hybrid approach allows the system to classify five different diseases and assess malignancy risk, providing consistent performance across multiple tests.

The implementation of the system as a web-based platform provides a user-friendly, portable, and accessible tool for healthcare providers and the public, potentially improving healthcare access and early diagnosis outcomes.

The study highlights the practicality of the system for real-world diagnostic use in settings with limited access to healthcare services, though it acknowledges the general challenge of distinguishing between melanoma and black nevi due to similar lesion characteristics.
important_remarks: The model struggles to distinguish between melanoma and melanocytic nevi (black nevi) due to similar lesion size and color characteristics, a limitation explicitly acknowledged by the authors.

The study lacks external validation; the classification model was trained on a Kaggle dataset while the detection model used primary data collected from coastal communities, raising concerns about dataset bias and generalizability.
No formal statistical hypothesis testing (e.g., p-values, confidence intervals) was performed; performance was evaluated using descriptive metrics (accuracy, mAP) and functional testing.

The dataset sizes are relatively small (5,000 images for classification, 3,000 for detection), which may limit the robustness of the deep learning models and their ability to generalize to broader populations.
The paper does not report sensitivity, specificity, or false positive/negative rates, which are critical metrics for clinical diagnostic tools, relying instead on accuracy and mAP.
The system is designed for resource-limited settings but relies on a web-based platform, which may face deployment challenges in areas with poor internet connectivity.
The study does not address demographic diversity (e.g., skin tone, ethnicity) in the dataset, which is a significant factor in skin disease diagnosis and a potential source of bias.

The detection task simplifies the problem by categorizing lesions only as 'Cancer' or 'Non-cancer', rather than providing specific diagnoses for malignant lesions, limiting clinical utility for precise treatment planning.
